hero.question: US x Iran meeting by April 10, 2026?
hero.prob_take: The crowd is pricing a US-Iran meeting by April 2026 at 1-in-3 odds, which feels like they're betting on either a major diplomatic shift or are just anchoring to "anything is possible in 14 months." The +19 point spike in 24 hours screams someone loaded the YES side on news, but there's zero evidence of actual backdoor talks, and both administrations have more incentive to posture than negotiate right now.

daily_take.headline: Iran Talks Priced Like Fantasy. Market Just Woke Up to Reality.
daily_take.deck: US-Iran meeting odds jumped 19 points to 30% in 24 hours on what looks like thin air. No major news catalyst. No Trump statement. No Tehran signal. The market is pricing in diplomatic momentum that doesn't exist yet. Smart money should ask what triggered this move before chasing it higher. Either traders know something, or they're front-running a narrative that collapses post-April.

sidebar.headline: Vance meetings are the real tell, not the principal. Watch if his 25% odds pull further away from the US-Iran 30% number.
